### Post by 4Real on 2007-07-29
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf(5) manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/share/fonts/X11/75dpi"
	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"i2c"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"vesa"
	BusID		"PCI:0:15:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-51
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		1
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		4
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		8
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		15
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		16
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth		24
		Modes		"1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Mode	0666
EndSection
________________________________________________________________________________________

I want 1024x768 SR ... Theres only options for 800X600 and 640x480 ... is someone could edit this that would be cool.

### Post by livingtarget on 2007-07-29
Little bit more info please. What would you like it to be edited to? If you use 7.04 I'd recommend installing the restricted drivers for your graphics card. You can use the System -> Administration -> Restricted Drivers Manager tool to enable drivers for your video card. I believe this is the easiest way.

Hope this helps. This should be easier than editing it manually.

Here's a quick pointer I can shoot your way:
Section "Device"
Identifier "Generic Video Card"
Driver "vesa" <=== This is the driver you are using atm, by changing this to the other drivers you can get 3d acceleration (but not recommended to mess with if you are a new to ubuntu.) You can easily get stuck in the terminal when you reboot if you do this wrong.
BusID "PCI:0:15:0"
EndSection

### Post by RudolfMDLT on 2007-07-29
I have no problem with helping you but what do you want edited? LOL! :)

To help you out, give us the following info:

1) You graphics card model
2) your desired Resolution you would like to run.
3) What version of ubuntu are you running!?

Thanks,

Rudolf

### Post by Vague on 2007-07-29
Well, since you asked in the other thread, I suppose I'll take a guess.  I think your HorizSync and VertRefresh values are probably wrong.  You may be able to identify the proper values by going to the terminal and entering ```
xresprobe vesa
```  I doubt that'll work for you, but you can try it and see if you get anything.

I would just reconfigure your X.org.  To do that, first go to the terminal and enter ```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.old
``` so that you'll have your current settings to copy back if your reconfiguration. . . doesn't go as planned.  Then enter ```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
``` and make sure you tell it to run at the resolution you want it to.  Oh yeah, and as everyone else pointed out, it'd be nice to know what graphics card you've got, so you can get the right driver going in there.

---

### Post by dmizer on 2007-07-29
the conf file you posted is just a configure.  since it's not showing 1024x768, then your hardware is not set up correctly.  and you've posted nothing about your hardware.

sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg and walk through the menus.  don't change anything until you get to the monitor selection stuff.

here's a nice howto with screenshots and all: [http://www.psychocats.net/ubuntu/xorg](http://www.psychocats.net/ubuntu/xorg)

### Post by RudolfMDLT on 2007-07-29

Ubuntu 7.0.4

What card did you buy? Is it a laptop? Please give a, if you can, your full PC spec that you bought.

 in the terminal type this:

**sudo apt-get install hardinfo**

It's a very easy to use hardware info interface.

once it has been installed, type in the terminal, **hardinfo** nad press enter.

Under PCI devices, there will be a sub heading, VGA compatible controller,  look for something that says either ATI, NVIDIA , SIS or Intel in this line and post back the exact details of that line.


**@HymnToLife**
Trminal 1, GUI 0

### Post by Bachstelze on 2007-07-29
The second command I told you will let you change the res. Use the space bar to select the one(s) you want and press Enter when you're done. Then save your work and press Ctrl+Alt+BkSp to restart X.

---

### Post by RudolfMDLT on 2007-07-29
Section "Device"
Identifier "Generic Video Card"
**Driver "vesa"**
BusID "PCI:0:15:0"
EndSection

find that line and change VESA to mga.

but before you do - are you comfortable using BASH? ie text commands because if the xorg file gets stuffed, x won't start. Do you have a backup PC that has access to the web?

otherwise(rather do this!)

open up the terminal, and type in 

**sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg**

When you go to select a driver, search for mga.

then select the res you need. Xorg wil default to the highest res so don't select them all, only the one you want!

### Post by ayenack on 2007-07-29
make a backup of your xorg.conf

sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf_backup

Then when you make any changes to your xorg.conf if it goes wrong you can simply re-install the backup. Like this.

sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf_backup /etc/X11/xorg.conf

Now you can try your new setup.

---

### Post by RudolfMDLT on 2007-07-29
after each reconfiguration you need to restart the xserver. ctrl + alt + backspace. note, this will kill all open apps! or you could just restart the PC. if after the restart, it still doesn't work please post the output of you xorg config file and the result of these commands:

lsmod | grep mga

lsmod | grep vesa

goodluck,

Rudolf

---

### Post by meierfra on 2007-07-29
You need a space in the middle of the command, like this:

```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf  /etc/X11/xorg.conf_backup
```

---

### Post by RudolfMDLT on 2007-07-29
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.con[COLOR="Red"]f/[/COLOR]etc/X11/xorg.conf_backup

you are missing a vital space.
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf_backup
